schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC), a Government of Andhra Pradesh undertaking, is announcing the "Garuda Scheme for Funeral Expenses" (GS- FE) for the poor Brahmins in Andhra Pradesh. Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹10,000/- shall be provided to meet the Funeral Expenses of the deceased Brahmin, through the prescribed process. This scheme covers Andhra Pradesh all 13 districts, to meet the funeral expenses of the deceased Brahmi.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - Financial Assistance of ₹10,000/- for funeral expenses.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The "Garuda Scheme for Funeral Expenses" aims to provide financial assistance for funeral expenses of a deceased Brahmin in their family, where the family has no other means to meet the same. This scheme covers Andhra Pradesh all 13 districts, to meet the funeral expenses of the deceased Brahmi.

children.text: Andhra Pradesh Brahmin Welfare Corporation (ABC), a Government of Andhra Pradesh undertaking, is announcing the "Garuda Scheme for Funeral Expenses" (GS- FE) for the poor Brahmins in Andhra Pradesh. Under this scheme, financial assistance of ₹10,000/- shall be provided to meet the Funeral Expenses of the deceased Brahmin, through the prescribed process. This scheme covers Andhra Pradesh all 13 districts, to meet the funeral expenses of the deceased Brahmi.
Financial Assistance of ₹10,000/- for funeral expenses.
Registration and apply
Step 01:
Applicants have to visit the official website(https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#) and click on "Scheme"
Step 02:
Click on "Registration" After that application form will open
Step 03:
Fill out the required details:
Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etc
Upload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details
Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etc
Finalize and "Submit" the application.
Step-4:
After submission receive the reference number.
Check Application Status:
Step 01:
Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "Know your status".
Step 02:
Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status.
Step 03:
Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details.
Download the Acknowledgement slip:
Step 01:
Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip"
Step 02:
Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme"
Step 03:
Click on the "Submit"

applicationProcess.process_md:
> **Registration and apply** **Step 01:** Applicants have to visit the official website(https://www.andhrabrahmin.ap.gov.in/schemes/schemes.aspx#) and click on "Scheme" **Step 02:** Click on "Registration" After that application form will open **Step 03:** Fill out the required details: - Personal Details: Name, Residence address, Aadhaar no, Ration card no, Bank detail etc - Upload: Photo, Signature and Certificate Details - Documents: Birth Certificate, Residential Certificate, Aadhar Card, Caste Certificate, Bank Passbook etc - Finalize and "Submit" the application. **Step-4:** After submission receive the reference number.
> **Check Application Status:** **Step 01:** Click on the “Services” link on the menu and select "Know your status". **Step 02:** Enter the Reference or Mobile or Aadhaar Card Number to get the application status. **Step 03:** Select the "Financial Year" and Fetch the details.
> **Download the Acknowledgement slip:** **Step 01:** Click on the "Acknowledgement Slip" **Step 02:** Enter the Aadhaar Card Number and select the "Scheme" **Step 03:** Click on the "Submit"

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md:
1. The applicant should be a close relative of the deceased (Spouse/Son/Daughter/Parent/Brother/Grandson). 
1. The applicant should provide a self-declaration that he/she is the legal heir of the deceased.
1. The applicant should belong to the Brahmin community and reside in Andhra Pradesh.
1. The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹75,000/-.
1. The applicant should not have availed himself/ herself of any amount under any other government scheme for the same purpose.

children.text: The applicant should be a close relative of the deceased (Spouse/Son/Daughter/Parent/Brother/Grandson).
The applicant should provide a self-declaration that he/she is the legal heir of the deceased.
The applicant should belong to the Brahmin community and reside in Andhra Pradesh.
The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹75,000/-.
The applicant should not have availed himself/ herself of any amount under any other government scheme for the same purpose.
